posts.text: Gregor Ojstersek reposted thisGregor Ojstersek • YouCTO | Founder of Engineering Leadership newsletter (188k+ subscribers) - Helping you become a great engineering leader!View my newsletter10h • Product thinking is the superpower of great engineers. They don't just focus on the technical solution, but also having a sense of: - ownership, - business value, - solving customers' problems.

Gregor Ojstersek reposted thisGregor Ojstersek • YouCTO | Founder of Engineering Leadership newsletter (188k+ subscribers) - Helping you become a great engineering leader!View my newsletter1d • One of the clearest signs of seniority in engineering is being comfortable saying “I don’t know” without losing the room.A lot of engineers often feel pressure to always have the answer. The belief is that credibility comes from knowing things. But credibility actually comes from judgment.The best engineers I’ve worked with don’t pretend they "know everything". They ask questions, challenge assumptions, and openly admit uncertainty when it exists.Because they value accuracy more than ego. And that creates trust.Learn more in this Engineering Leadership article: https://lnkd.in/erdeDUXM

Gregor Ojstersek • YouCTO | Founder of Engineering Leadership newsletter (188k+ subscribers) - Helping you become a great engineering leader!View my newsletter1d • One of the clearest signs of seniority in engineering is being comfortable saying “I don’t know” without losing the room.A lot of engineers often feel pressure to always have the answer. The belief is that credibility comes from knowing things. But credibility actually comes from judgment.The best engineers I’ve worked with don’t pretend they "know everything". They ask questions, challenge assumptions, and openly admit uncertainty when it exists.Because they value accuracy more than ego. And that creates trust.Learn more in this Engineering Leadership article: https://lnkd.in/erdeDUXM

Gregor Ojstersek reposted thisGregor Ojstersek • YouCTO | Founder of Engineering Leadership newsletter (188k+ subscribers) - Helping you become a great engineering leader!View my newsletter2d • Code review process is not made for the AI era.The problem a lot of teams are facing these days is the following:How to scale the code reviews in a way that would support the speed of generation of the newly created code, without generating bad code, e.g. “AI code slop”.And these are the 2 extremes I am seeing:1. Teams block the progress by doing great human code reviews and ensuring that the quality remains high2. Teams rely just on AI code reviews and/or doing shallow human code reviewsOne of the ways to solve this is by moving the review process earlier, even before the first line of code is written.Focusing on building a great specification and reviewing it thoroughly before prompting an AI to generate the code.Learn more in the latest Engineering Leadership article: https://lnkd.in/dQd7aYqm

Gregor Ojstersek • YouCTO | Founder of Engineering Leadership newsletter (188k+ subscribers) - Helping you become a great engineering leader!View my newsletter2d • Code review process is not made for the AI era.The problem a lot of teams are facing these days is the following:How to scale the code reviews in a way that would support the speed of generation of the newly created code, without generating bad code, e.g. “AI code slop”.And these are the 2 extremes I am seeing:1. Teams block the progress by doing great human code reviews and ensuring that the quality remains high2. Teams rely just on AI code reviews and/or doing shallow human code reviewsOne of the ways to solve this is by moving the review process earlier, even before the first line of code is written.Focusing on building a great specification and reviewing it thoroughly before prompting an AI to generate the code.Learn more in the latest Engineering Leadership article: https://lnkd.in/dQd7aYqm

Gregor Ojstersek reposted thisGregor Ojstersek • YouCTO | Founder of Engineering Leadership newsletter (188k+ subscribers) - Helping you become a great engineering leader!View my newsletter3d • Tech Lead is becoming an increasingly important role due to GenAI.The reason?A good mix of technical and people-related skills, which includes:- common sense and pragmatism,- good communication and leadership,- being a multiplier for others and making everyone around them better.These skills do well in the AI era and are just going to be more and more important.To learn more, read this Engineering Leadership article: https://lnkd.in/eKeXRZFJ
